description: Retrieval strategy catalog for RAG Studio. Defines how documents are retrieved from the vector store.

strategies.name: Similarity Search
strategies.description: Retrieves the top-K documents with the highest cosine (or dot product) similarity to the query embedding. The simplest and fastest retrieval method.
pros: Simple, well-understood semantics
Fastest retrieval method
Works out of the box with any vector store
Predictable behavior
cons: Pure semantic match — misses keyword matches

parameters.name: scoreThreshold
parameters.description: Minimum similarity score to include a document (null = no threshold)

strategies.name: Maximum Marginal Relevance (MMR)
strategies.description: Balances relevance and diversity. Iteratively selects documents that are relevant to the query but dissimilar to already-selected documents. Reduces redundancy in retrieved context.
pros: Reduces redundant context passed to LLM
Improves coverage of different aspects
Better for complex multi-part questions
Reduces hallucination from repetitive context
cons: May miss the single most relevant document
More complex to implement and explain

parameters.name: topK
parameters.description: Final number of documents to return

parameters.name: fetchK
parameters.description: Number of documents to fetch before MMR selection

strategies.name: Hybrid Search
strategies.description: Combines dense semantic search with sparse keyword search (BM25). Merges results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion or weighted scoring. Best of both worlds.
bestFor: Legal documents with exact phrase matching
When keyword and semantic signals both matter
pros: Catches both semantic meaning AND exact keywords
Significantly better recall for specific terms
More robust than pure vector search
Industry standard for production RAG
cons: Requires sparse index (BM25) in addition to dense

parameters.name: topK
parameters.description: Number of documents to return after fusion

strategies.name: Parent-Child Retrieval
strategies.description: Indexes small child chunks for precision retrieval, but returns their larger parent chunks to the LLM for richer context. Combines retrieval precision with generation context.
pros: High precision retrieval (small chunks)
Rich context for generation (large parent chunks)
Reduces hallucination by providing more context
Better answer completeness
cons: More complex to set up and maintain
Larger context windows needed for the LLM

parameters.name: parentChunkSize
parameters.description: Token size for parent chunks (returned to LLM)

parameters.name: topK
parameters.description: Number of parent chunks to return

strategies.name: Multi-Query Retrieval
strategies.description: Uses an LLM to generate multiple query variants from the user's original question. Retrieves documents for each variant and takes the union. Improves recall for ambiguous queries.
bestFor: When recall is more important than precision
pros: Dramatically improves recall for complex queries
Handles rephrasing and synonym variations
Finds relevant docs the original query missed
Good for open-ended research questions
cons: Adds LLM call latency (extra round trip per query)
May retrieve noisy or tangentially related docs

parameters.name: numQueries
parameters.description: Number of query variants to generate

parameters.name: topK
parameters.description: Documents to retrieve per query variant

strategies.name: Ensemble (RRF)
strategies.description: Runs multiple retrieval strategies in parallel and combines results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). Each strategy contributes to the final ranking. Best recall at cost of latency.
bestFor: When no single strategy is clearly best
When evaluation shows mixed retrieval quality
pros: Best possible recall — captures docs any single strategy misses
Robust to individual strategy weaknesses
No single point of failure in retrieval
Proven in production at large scale

parameters.name: topK
parameters.description: Final number of documents after RRF fusion
